subHeadline: The verdicts people actually want before they read a single spec sheet — the calls we'd make for a friend, backed by real UK enquiry and failure data.

rankings.why: Consistently outperforms its diesel sibling in our data — water pump replacement is the only meaningful weak point.
Strong retained values and a clean ✅ Safe verdict across every engine variant — no caveat in the data at all.
A well-valued executive saloon with the revised Ingenium range and replacement viable across almost every example.
Shared with Land Rover's Discovery 3 — often kills the engine outright with little warning. £5,000–£7,500 recon.
Compact SUV values comfortably absorb the modern Ingenium's replacement cost — safe across the board.
Rapid depreciation plus genuine reliability concerns on pre-2007 cars — replacement cost frequently exceeds vehicle value.
The only genuinely practical SUV in the range, paired with the strongest ownership-safety profile of any Jaguar.
Flagship values comfortably absorb even the AJ133's upper recon cost — the keeper's Jaguar.

rows.claim: Engine failure data verified by independent specialists

rows.source: Aggregated from enquiry volume, failure frequency, and replacement economics
